title: Meeting Rooms
description:
Given an array of meeting time interval objects consisting of start and end times `[[start_1,end_1],[start_2,end_2],...] (start_i < end_i)`, determine if a person could add all meetings to their schedule without any conflicts.

Note: (0,8),(8,10) is not considered a conflict at 8

examples.explanation:
* `(0,30)` and `(5,10)` will conflict
* `(0,30)` and `(15,20)` will conflict

hint_plan.level_1: Break down the core operation in Meeting Rooms.
hint_plan.level_2: Look for the data structure or traversal pattern that avoids repeated work.
hint_plan.level_3: Use the constraints and examples to narrow the intended time complexity.
hint_plan.level_4: Implement the direct approach that maintains the required invariant step by step.

canonical_solution_summary: Implement the direct approach that maintains the required invariant step by step.

company_track_tags.reason: practical DSA, heaps, intervals, sliding window, fast tradeoffs
clean implementation, readability, arrays, strings, trees
